post_rendered:
WELCOME TO MAFIA GAME 178: JESTER MAFIA

credit to e_is_cool for most of the rules

setup
When one faction achieves their win condition, the game ends

Role PMs text (the greetings were made up on the spot):
2 mimes
You win if both you and your partner, [name], are hanged. You are not able to chat during day or the night.

2 mafia goons
You win if both you and your partner, [name] control majority for the game. You have access to 24/7 chat, here: [quicktopic]. You both also have a shared factional kill, and can target one person every night.

2 masons
You win when both mafia are eliminated. You have 24/7 chat with your partner, [name], here: [quicktopic]

5 VT
You win if both mafia are eliminated.

When 1 mime is nightkilled, all other mimes die as well


voting and hangs
  • vote only using Vote: name make sure everything is correct
  • invalid votes are ignored
  • dont do anything stupid in vote technicalities
  • if tie first person to get that number of votes is hanged
  • if there are no votes in a day then i win
  • no majority day 1
  • deadlines are called deadlines for a reason
  • incorrect votecounts will be counted as correct if not corrected in 24 hours
  • this game is on deadline hang. The person with the most number of votes at the end of each day is hanged. In case of a tie, at random will be hanged. On day 2+, if a player receives the most number of votes, they will be hanged.


activity and deadlines
  • 14/3 phase periods
  • be active every 24 hours plz
  • prod dodges arent considered being active
  • deadline is at 9pm et
  • deadlines may be extended at my discretion
  • 24 after pm prod = replacement


basic rules
  • no confirm rule
  • dont post if not in game
  • dont post at night
  • dont oot
  • you can edit/delete posts. Don't edit posts if dead
  • you can conceal text
  • dont post if dead
  • dont be immature
  • dont argue with me
  • dont do anything stupid
  • ask for rule clarifications in PM

dead(7)

zdtsd, Vanilla Townie,dies during D3.
NDMath, Town Mason, and kevinmathz, Mafia Goon, have been modkilled

significant events

The Mime faction has lost.
jeffisepic has declared that day 2 ends 3 hours after his next calculus exam.
No hanging results in an instant town loss.
No more editing or deleting posts

Night communication allowed during Night 2

post_rendered:
Hi, I'm kevin and one of your pretty experienced players for this game. Feel free to ask me or any of the other experienced players any questions. Don't ask people who aren't in the game though, there are some scrubs like e who will probably give you bad advice

PSA: If you haven't already I highly recommend you read a past game. Helps out quite a bit

Also sometime in the day we should mason code

Example of a mason code: M~ ~~~~n~~e ~~ ~~~i~~~~h~, ~~d ~ ~~ ~ ~~~o~. ~~ ~~r~~e~ ~~ ~d~~~.

When you claim mason and your partner is dead, enter the missing blanks. For example, if I was mason and I sent the above message to zdtsd with the blanks filled in, he could claim mason and enter my blanks as "My username is kevinmathz, and I am a mason. My partner is zdtsd."

This way, when one mason dies and the other is about to be hanged, the other mason can claim and be cleared like that, and lead town. Masons can also soft.

You guys should all do that kind of stuff, called "coding". I urge you all to make good codes.

post_rendered:
hey, yelling at people about how they're playing the game wrong is more important than iso'ing madein


define "useful". nearly everything i do is "useful", but about half the time it's just not obvious. i recommend (if you have the time) to read my article on scumhunting for elaboration.
wait i think the first two sentences of this response were almost exactly something i said in 170 :thonk:

there's always a reason behind tone being good or bad; calling tone horrible with no context means you don't want to take the time to flesh out the case. like, tone isn't horrible because it's horrible - you might think a player has no gamesolving intention for making a post, a post sounds really awkward and seems indicative of a scum trying to fit in, et cetera. these are cases, "horrible tone" by itself is not. the reason why is that sometimes your idea of tone will actually be based off of something absurd like capitalization or something, and so actually digging into the root cause is the best way to make sure you're not pranking yourself.

okay i thought you knew how bandwagoning as a term worked but i guess not. a bandwagon is only noncontextually scummy if the reason for getting on the person is the same (two people can dislike the same player for completely different reasons, is there a problem with that?) and if the second player displays a lack of interest in thinking for themselves. so like, it's bad if i were to scream "yeah i agree with kevin to vote for madein for tiebreakers! vote: madein" but that's clearly not this.
instead of just applying buzzwords, i recommend actually thinking about why it should be scummy; that should be much more useful and accurate, as well as being the foundation for a more solid case in general

i'm pretty sure you're either falling into confirmation bias or confusing "being towny" with "having town equity" here; i think it's most likely the latter based off of your posts so i'll respond to that. i'll agree with you that i have little to no town equity, and that most people here do, in some way, shape, or form. but does having town equity imply you immediately must be town? clearly not, and particularly not if you have scum equity. i see your old "reasons for why X is town" post, and i'm assuming that's roughly what you're going off of, but i think you should know now the issues with calling tone good and leaving it at that.

fwiw, in my experience consensus hangs are usually terrible hangs (with the exception of policy hangs and mechanically outed people). it's very rare that everybody or almost everybody agrees on a read; when they do, a lot of the time it's because scum played some part in making that happen.

regarding taunting: i'm pretty sure this is referring to the time i said something like "please vote me if you find me scummy" (correct me if i'm wrong). i said this because it's really hard to get info when people respect you too much. i didn't intend for it to come off as "vote for me u wont" but the info doesn't really make a difference anyways
(fwiw, this kind of "taunting"-like playstyle is very similar to something i did in 170; tl;dr i spent the first half of day 1 openwolfing there as town and proceeded to solve the game in the second half with all the info i got. it's a legit strategy, basically.)

regarding madein: i see no reason here why the "bandwagoning" is scummy. your case is on why madein is town, which has nothing to do with the argument at hand.

the problem with what you're saying with the "no scum equity" is that after this statement, it seems to me like you're just not looking. like, you talk about how you can imagine things coming from town (for ahir for instance); sure, i'm not gonna say it's impossible, cuz nothing's truly impossible. but the likelihoods are incomparable.
i could be wrong on this, perhaps. but i really do feel like you're townclearing people too easily and not evaluating them after that, which is not a recipe for success.

Regarding masons and wagons: the difference between mafia and masons here is that mafia typically try to push some form of sinister agenda, while masons are just doing normal town stuff with the benefit of being masons. Basically, that means Mafia can "draw conclusions" that fit whatever they want to accomplish, which is why a lot of times with huge wagons, at least one Mafia has decided to help the wagon along.
None of this is a certainty, of course, but it pays to be cautious in this game, and consensus hangs are one thing that should give cause for reevaluation (even if it doesn't pull anything up, just actually doing it is soooooo helpful)

going into f6 with two masons and one scum gives town a confirmed win. have both masons claim, hang two non-masons at random, one of the masons die, hang the other two non-masons during f3.
assuming there are two of them still alive, having masons claim at f6 is probably correct regardless, because hitting one scum on that day still gives town a mechanical win. (obviously if a mason is killed tonight, don't have the other one claim)
similarly, f3 can be done by deciding on a top town and putting the other people at 1 vote each. if there's a mason alive then f3 is an instant town win regardless - mason claims, if there are two mason claims then they crossvote without even worrying about mason codes and all that stuff

assuming there are two of them still alive, having masons claim at f6 is probably correct regardless, because hitting one scum on that day still gives town a mechanical win. (obviously if a mason is killed tonight, don't have the other one claim)
similarly, f3 can be done by deciding on a top town and putting the other people at 1 vote each. if there's a mason alive then f3 is an instant town win regardless - mason claims, if there are two mason claims then they crossvote without even worrying about mason codes and all that stuff

post_rendered:
the setup itself
This game was mostly experimental to test out many many different mechanics. There were obviously things that went wrong, and I needed to hastily fix.

Things that went wrong: Role PM fiasco, mason claiming fiasco, double hanging/no deadline. The first 2 were partially my fault for not specifying in the rules. The double hanging things didn't work, as it was way too townsided in the end.

Things that went smoothly: nighttalk, mimes. Nighttalk didn't impact the game too much, but it adds additional reactions/information from and about people that can be interesting. In my opinion, the setup was actual mostly balanced, and seems pretty fun and cool at that too.


the play
I was actually very impressed with the game. I'll cut to the point: NDMath, hdgehrke, zdtsd, Made_in_2004, mathjay all portrayed SE level play, the screw with the masons was more of a mechanical issue then anything. He showed leadership, and was on point most of the time too. Kevinmathz's fakeclaim was quite nice, but the rest of his play was lurky at best.
Mathjay, despite using emotion to his side at times, played very steadily and reasonably. The performance by Made_in_2004 shows people to not underestimate his wolf game anymore.
